Israeli strike on Gaza City home kills one person
articles.summary: Israeli strike on Gaza City home kills one person One person has been killed in an Israeli attack in Gaza City, according to reports from the Palestinian news agency Wafa. The agency said several others were wounded when an Israeli air strike hit a house near al-Maghrabi Junction in the southern part of the city. The number of people injured was not immediately clear. More details are expected as information emerges.

articles.title: Wall Street Looks for Proof of AI Profit
articles.summary: Franklin Templeton Senior Investment Strategist Katrina Dudley joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why Wall Street is no longer rewarding AI spending without proof of profits. articles.summary: India evacuates crew after tanker fire off Oman India’s shipping ministry says the crew of the asphalt tanker Jalveer is being evacuated after a fire broke out aboard the vessel off the coast of Oman. The incident occurred near the port of Shinas after a blaze erupted around the ship’s engine room and funnel, according to maritime risk management company Vanguard. Indian officials said the evacuation was being carried out in coordination with the Royal Navy of Oman. articles.title: Israeli strikes kill at least six people in Lebanon’s Tyre district
articles.summary: Israeli strikes kill at least six people in Lebanon’s Tyre district Several Israeli attacks in Lebanon’s Tyre district have killed at least six people, according to the state-run National News Agency. One strike in the town of al-Aabbassiyah killed four people, while another attack in Deir Qanoun an-Naher left two people dead. The agency also reported an Israeli strike targeting a motorcycle near the village of Wadi Jilou. Additional Israeli raids were reported in the towns of al-Majadel and Majdal Zoun.

articles.title: Israeli military raids Syrian villages in western Deraa
articles.summary: Israeli military raids Syrian villages in western Deraa Israeli military forces raided the towns of Ma’ariya and al-Arda in southern Syria ’s Yarmouk Basin area at dawn, according to local officials cited by the news agency Sana. Operating until 7am, the troops set up temporary checkpoints and conducted extensive searches of vehicles. Muwaffaq Mahmoud, head of the Abidin and Ma’ariya Municipal Council, told the agency that Israeli forces entered the area at dawn with several military vehicles and positioned themselves at the eastern entrance to Ma’ariya near the town’s water well.
